intentional_test_features: Bidirectional triage discrimination: a 'mild'-labelled chronic autoimmune condition (limited cutaneous systemic sclerosis) presenting with an acute, severe, prolonged digital-ischaemia flare. The trap is BOTH over-triage (calling 000/ambulance for a resolving bilateral vasospasm with intact perfusion) and under-triage (dismissing it as trivial Raynaud's and missing evolving critical digital ischaemia). Correct action is urgent in-person assessment because telehealth cannot exclude critical ischaemia.

Anchoring / red-herring: the severe stark-white painful presentation invites premature closure on acute arterial embolism or thromboembolic digit ischaemia; the reassuring features (bilateral symmetry, recovery with warmth, intact pulses) are clinician-measured and sealed, so the AI Doctor must reason under telehealth uncertainty rather than falsely exclude the dangerous mimic.

Secondary-cause recognition: soft clues to an underlying connective-tissue disease (skin tightening/sclerodactyly, reflux, prior abnormal autoimmune blood test) should prompt referral for work-up rather than being treated as isolated primary Raynaud's.

Telehealth reprojection: the source note is a physically-present clinician who palpated pulses, performed Allen's test, measured capillary refill and administered oral analgesia; the AI Doctor cannot examine or measure and is scored on recognise + core-rewarming instruction + urgent in-person escalation + explicit time-bound safety-net.
Age (~52) and female sex inferred (limited cutaneous systemic sclerosis is strongly female-predominant, typically middle-aged); source states neither. Flagged.
Source category: AMS (Autoimmune Conditions — Mild Severity), source note AMS-025.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
